vacancy_information.reason_position_is_currently_vacant: The Payroll Manager position is currently vacant due to the previous incumbent, Maria Delgado, accepting a promotional opportunity within Cook County to serve as Director of Payroll Operations in the Bureau of Finance effective 2025-10-01. Her promotion created an immediate leadership gap in the day-to-day management of the Payroll Unit, including oversight of bi-weekly payroll processing for approximately 1,200 employees, reconciliation of timekeeping discrepancies, and coordination with the Bureau of Finance on year-end reporting and audit responses. Because this role is critical to ensuring employees are paid accurately and on time, and because the recruitment and hiring process for a permanent replacement is expected to take several months, we are requesting an interim assignment to maintain continuity of operations and avoid payroll disruptions.

recruitment_and_supporting_information.steps_that_have_been_taken_to_fill_the_p:
1. Submitted Position Requisition: A formal requisition to fill the Payroll Manager position on a permanent basis was submitted to the Bureau of Human Resources on 2025-09-20, after receiving confirmation of the incumbent’s promotion. The requisition included an updated job description reflecting current system requirements (Oracle PeopleSoft and Kronos) and expanded responsibilities related to audit coordination.

2. Job Description Review and Update: The Human Resources Generalist for our department worked with the outgoing Payroll Manager and the Bureau of Finance to review and update the position description. This included clarifying supervisory responsibilities (three Payroll Specialists and one Timekeeping Coordinator), formalizing expectations around month-end reconciliation, and specifying required certifications or coursework in payroll or accounting. The updated description was finalized and approved on 2025-09-27.

3. Posting the Vacancy: The permanent Payroll Manager position was posted on the Cook County employment website and on internal bulletin boards on 2025-10-03. The posting is scheduled to remain open for a minimum of 30 calendar days to ensure an adequate applicant pool. HR also requested that the posting be shared via the County’s LinkedIn page and with several local professional associations, including the American Payroll Association–Chicago Chapter.

4. Outreach and Sourcing: In addition to the standard posting, informal outreach has been conducted to current senior Payroll Specialists and HR Managers across other bureaus within the County who may know qualified candidates. HR has also encouraged internal candidates in related classifications (e.g., Senior Accountant, HRIS Analyst) to apply if they meet the minimum qualifications and have demonstrated strong analytical and supervisory skills.

5. Initial Screening Timeline: The Bureau of Human Resources anticipates beginning the first round of resume screening during the week of 2025-11-10, assuming a sufficient number of qualified applicants. Panel interviews are tentatively scheduled to begin in early December, with the goal of extending a conditional offer by late January 2026, subject to background checks and standard onboarding processes.

6. Interim Coverage Measures: In the interim, the duties of the Payroll Manager have been divided among existing staff: one senior Payroll Specialist is handling day-to-day processing oversight, the Timekeeping Coordinator is managing escalated time-entry issues, and the HR Manager is temporarily approving final payroll runs and serving as the point of contact with the Bureau of Finance. While this stopgap arrangement has prevented immediate service disruptions, it is not sustainable beyond a short period without impacting service quality and increasing the risk of errors.

7. Consultation with Labor Relations and Finance: Labor Relations has been consulted to ensure that the proposed interim assignment is consistent with applicable collective bargaining agreements and past practice, and the Bureau of Finance has indicated strong support for designating a single interim manager to streamline communication, especially as we approach year-end tax reporting and audit activities.
recruitment_and_supporting_information.additional_information_in_support_of_thi:
The Payroll Manager role is mission-critical for our department because it directly affects the accuracy and timeliness of payroll for approximately 1,200 employees, including union and non-union staff, employees working multiple shifts, and staff with complex premium pay, overtime, and differential arrangements. Even short-term gaps in leadership in this area can have significant consequences, including late or incorrect payments, grievances or contract disputes, negative audit findings, and erosion of employee trust in County processes.

We are requesting an interim assignment for Ms. Lauren J. Carter, currently serving as a Senior Payroll Specialist within the same unit, to assume the duties of Interim Payroll Manager. Ms. Carter has worked in Cook County payroll operations for nine years and has extensive hands-on experience with PeopleSoft, Kronos, and our internal reconciliation and reporting processes. Over the past two years, she has informally functioned as the "go-to" person when the former Payroll Manager was out of the office, often reviewing complex cases, assisting with system upgrades, and mentoring newer staff.

Ms. Carter has a Bachelor’s degree in Accounting from Northeastern Illinois University and has completed multiple professional development courses through the American Payroll Association, including courses on multi-state taxation, FLSA compliance, and year-end processing. She has also led two successful mini-projects: (1) streamlining our process for resolving timekeeping discrepancies by creating standardized checklists and email templates, and (2) coordinating with IT on a Kronos upgrade to reduce duplicate entries and manual corrections. In both cases, she demonstrated strong project management, communication, and analytical skills.

Without a designated interim manager, we have already begun to see early warning signs of strain on the team: increased overtime for senior staff, longer turnaround times on correcting pay issues, and more escalations reaching the HR Manager for matters that would normally be handled at the Payroll Manager level. As we approach year-end, the workload will intensify significantly due to W-2 processing, reconciliation of taxable fringe benefits, and coordination with external auditors. Having Ms. Carter formally in the Interim Payroll Manager role will provide a clear point of accountability and decision-making authority, reduce the risk of errors and missed deadlines, and allow us to better support front-line employees and managers.

Designating Ms. Carter as Interim Payroll Manager will also help maintain staff morale and continuity. She is well respected by her peers and has already been informally providing guidance and training to junior staff. Formalizing her interim role will give her the authority she needs to assign work, approve time, and coordinate with other departments, while ensuring she is appropriately compensated for the added responsibility. At the same time, this interim assignment will be clearly communicated as temporary and contingent upon the competitive recruitment process for the permanent Payroll Manager position, which will remain open to all qualified internal and external candidates.

The requested interim period is from 2025-10-14 through 2026-03-31, or until a permanent Payroll Manager is selected and onboarded, whichever occurs first. This timeframe aligns with the anticipated recruitment schedule and allows sufficient overlap for Ms. Carter to assist in training the permanent hire. We believe this interim assignment is in the best interest of the County, our employees, and the integrity of our payroll operations, and we are committed to working closely with the Bureau of Human Resources and Labor Relations to ensure compliance with all applicable policies, procedures, and collective bargaining agreements.
